##### ↳ ↳ Re: [OT] 'Obvious' is in the Mind of the Beholder

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com
- **Date:** 2004-10-23T05:54:21-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<cld9od$h4l$1@panix5.panix.com>`
- **References:** `<clb6kv$ei9$1@panix5.panix.com> <2tte7nF23t93aU1@uni-berlin.de>`

```
In article <2tte7nF23t93aU1@uni-berlin.de>,
Pete Dashwood <dashwood@enternet.co.nz> wrote:
>
><docdwarf@panix.com> wrote in message news:clb6kv$ei9$1@panix5.panix.com...
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
>Given no context, I'm not sure what this is about, Doc.

Why, it's about ten lines long, give or take a few.

>
>I took it to be hex arithmetic.
>
>((base-1) + 1 = base)

Now take a step back... you learned hex a few years ago and having done 
such associate a particular interpretation with the symbols I posted.

These abilities (learning hex, learning the forms of arithmetic 
statements, learning the Roman alphabet, etc) allow you to look at the 
series of symbols

F + 1 = 10

... and say 'This is obvious/clear.'

>
>Is there a point I'm missing here?

Only that the clarity does not lie in the statement but in the abilities 
the mind of the beholder brings to bear on it; one who had learned the 
forms of arithmetic statements and the Roman alphabet but had not learned 
hex math might have a response of 'What are you talking about... you 
cannot add letters and numbers... or maybe F + 1 = G, because G is one 
letter after F... or maybe it is an algebraic equation, solving for F 
gives 9... there are many possible answers and none is 'obvious' to me.'

DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: [OT] 'Obvious' is in the Mind of the Beholder

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "Pete Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2004-10-26T02:03:53+13:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2u4c95F26cr1aU1@uni-berlin.de>`

```
"YukonMama" <yukonmama@aol.com> wrote in message
news:20041025004923.26518.00002949@mb-m03.aol.com...
> >From: "Pete Dashwood" dashwood@enternet.co.nz
> >Date: 10/23/04 9:13 PM Eastern Daylight Time
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>
Then he picked up his hammer and saw...

"I hear", said the deaf man, but he couldn't really. So he gathered his cows
and herd...

"I speak", said the dumb man, but he couldn't really. So he picked up his
wheel and spoke...

"I smell", said the man with no nose, but he couldn't really, so he cast his
nets in the estuary, caught some fish and smelt...

"I feel", said the man with no nerves, but he couldn't really, so he picked
up his carpet and felt.

"I taste", said the man with no tongue, but he couldn't really, so he took
some tinzaparin and TAISTed.

("Obviously", neither the dumb man nor the man with no tongue REALLY said
anything... obviously...er...is it obvious?...I dunno... bloody Dwarf
starting these controversies...I thought it was obvious....)

Pete.
```

## Thread: WinAPI calls that use the Shell32.lib library — 16 msgs

### WinAPI calls that use the Shell32.lib library

- **From:** "Pete Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2004-10-23T18:40:07+13:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2tu95rF250m5iU1@uni-berlin.de>`

```
I've been trying to use some Windows API calls that utilise the Shell32.lib
and I keep getting unresolved externs in the link.

I had a look at the Fujitsu supplied libs, and Kernel32.lib and User32.lib
are there, but there is no Shell32.lib.

I found the entry points for the API calls I want to use in the f5ddapi.dll,
but there is no .lib with this for compiling.

It looks like it needs another approach.

Is there something special about the Shell32 library that I'm missing?

Anyone have any clues for getting API calls that are not  just "run of the
mill" Kernel32 stuff (as shown in the Fujitsu samples ) to link OK?

Thanks,

Pete.

PS I want to place an output text file onto the User's desktop, so I need
"SHGetSpecialFolderLocation" and "SHGetPathFromIDList"
```

#### ↳ Re: WinAPI calls that use the Shell32.lib library

- **From:** "Lorne Sunley" <lsunley@mb.sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2004-10-23T05:52:09+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<JMPlV5b5VFnN-pn2-p2e4vG9vEf40@S01060080c8f879e3.wp.shawcable.net>`
- **References:** `<2tu95rF250m5iU1@uni-berlin.de>`

```
On Sat, 23 Oct 2004 05:40:07 UTC, "Pete Dashwood" 
<dashwood@enternet.co.nz> wrote:

> I've been trying to use some Windows API calls that utilise the Shell32.lib
> and I keep getting unresolved externs in the link.
…[19 more quoted lines elided]…
> "SHGetSpecialFolderLocation" and "SHGetPathFromIDList"

You need the shell32.lib. Can you get a Windows SDK? All the libs 
should be in there.
```

#### ↳ Re: WinAPI calls that use the Shell32.lib library

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2004-10-23T18:11:16+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<89xed.13090$Rf1.3003@newssvr19.news.prodigy.com>`
- **References:** `<2tu95rF250m5iU1@uni-berlin.de>`

```
"Pete Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz> wrote in message
news:2tu95rF250m5iU1@uni-berlin.de...
> I've been trying to use some Windows API calls that utilise the
Shell32.lib
> and I keep getting unresolved externs in the link.
>
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> I found the entry points for the API calls I want to use in the
f5ddapi.dll,
> but there is no .lib with this for compiling.
>
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> mill" Kernel32 stuff (as shown in the Fujitsu samples ) to link OK?

What might be "special" about SHELL32.DLL is what I mention later.. that the
minimum required version may not be available on the user system.

That said, I don't know much (frankly, I know ZERO) about linking FJ
programs, but you can get at any function in a DLL using the kernel32.dll
functions LoadLibrary and GetProcAddress plus whatever mechanism you use for
"call by address".  (What does FJ link? LoadLibrary and GetProcAddress?. It
would be plumb dopey IMO to link the actual code, since that code is
available on most Windows systems).

> PS I want to place an output text file onto the User's desktop, so I need
> "SHGetSpecialFolderLocation" and "SHGetPathFromIDList"

Those functions are exported from SHELL32.DLL *** version 4.0 or greater
**** which version MAY OR MAY NOT BE AVAILABLE ON THE USER'S SYSTEM.
```

#### ↳ Re: WinAPI calls that use the Shell32.lib library

- **From:** "Pete Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2004-10-24T14:44:33+13:00

- **Message-ID:** `<2u0focF23ll58U1@uni-berlin.de>`
- **References:** `<2tu95rF250m5iU1@uni-berlin.de>`

```

"Pete Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz> wrote in message

Shell32.lib

f5ddapi.dll,
> but there is no .lib with this for compiling.
>
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Thanks to all who replied.

I still haven't sorted it.

Here's the "story so far"...

1. Realised Shell32.lib is not provided by Fujitsu with their NetCOBOL or
PowerCOBOL offerings. (Can't imagine why not, but I'm sure there's a good
reason...maybe, as Michael pointed out, this can vary so much across
different machines, they simply can't do it... don't know.)

2. Several posters suggested getting the SDK (last time I had an SDK was for
Windows 3.1.....ah, those were the days <G>) I went to MS to get it and it
is a 207MB download so that's out of the question on my 56K link. Could send
off for the disk, but by the time I get it, the application will be
irretrievably delayed. I simply need to solve this, now.

3. There are web sites that offer missing .dlls, maybe I can download a
.lib......searched, nothing.

4. I have Shell32.dll on my system, might it be possible to extract the
entry points into a .lib? Too difficult (for me, I'm sure some of you could
do this in minutes) in the time available.

5. Located on the web a tool that does exactly what I described in 4, above.
Downloaded it, ran it, Presto! I have a Shell32.lib file!

6. Attached the new lib into the NetCOBOL project, recompiled, same
thing...unresolved externs on the two entry points... Bugger!

I'll re-read Michael's post and see if I can understand it better. (It's not
you, Michael, it's me...I'm getting old... <G>)

Meantme, if anyone has a copy of Shell32.lib I'd sure be glad to try
it....<G>

Pete.
```

```
downloaded it. The whole download is 10 .cab files, 9 of
which are 25607KB. The tenth is only 8295kb. There are
also two other files, a bat file to do the extraction from
a cab, and their extraction program.

As you pointed out the whole thing is very large. On Sunday,
I shall try to unpack the whole thing, and if I see the file
you need, and the size is reasonable, and you agree, I'll attach
it to a message to you.

Warren
wsimmons5@optonline.net
```

- **From:** "Stephen Gennard" <stephen.gennard@someone.somewhere.at.a.m.focus.company.com>

```
You should beware...



The license agreement you agreed to before installing the software does not 
allow you to redistributing the shell32.lib file.



The only files you can redistribute are the ones stated in the license 
agreement (see "<install_dir>Microsoft SDK\License")
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: WinAPI calls that use the Shell32.lib library

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "Stephen Gennard" <stephen.gennard@someone.somewhere.at.a.m.focus.company.com>
- **Date:** 2004-10-27T09:33:29+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2u94qtF28htmrU1@uni-berlin.de>`
- **References:** `<2tu95rF250m5iU1@uni-berlin.de> <2u0focF23ll58U1@uni-berlin.de> <PnEed.53550$YM4.20835860@news4.srv.hcvlny.cv.net> <2u3uljF25aambU1@uni-berlin.de> <bcAfd.1366$I54.76@fe40.usenetserver.com>`

```
If the Microsoft Platform SDK is shipped in whole or you ship a component 
that is not part of the redistributable set then Microsoft expects you to 
pay a royalty payment for each SDK shipped.



Personally I think it is odd that you can tell your customers to download it 
for free but you can not distribute with your product unless you say such a 
royalty payment. but that's life!
```

```
Michael gave the answer. I'll just explain it in simpler terms. Lib is
for static link (old school); dll is dynamically bound (ahhh). Just
load the dll, get entry points into procedure pointers and call
through them.
```

#### ↳ Re: WinAPI calls that use the Shell32.lib library

- **From:** "Pete Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2004-10-25T02:08:49+13:00

```
I'm pleased to say this problem is now resolved, thanks to some help from a
member of CLC who mailed me
the .lib. He did it privately and hasn't authorised me to give his name so I
won't, but there are some very helpful and useful people frequenting this
forum.

I appreciate all the help from everybody and, just in case anybody else
wants to do this, I have posted the working debugged code below.

"Pete Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz> wrote in message
news:2tu95rF250m5iU1@uni-berlin.de...
> I've been trying to use some Windows API calls that utilise the
Shell32.lib
> and I keep getting unresolved externs in the link.
>
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> I found the entry points for the API calls I want to use in the
f5ddapi.dll,
> but there is no .lib with this for compiling.
>
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
> "SHGetSpecialFolderLocation" and "SHGetPathFromIDList"

The scenario is that, given an action flag, I want to scan a database and
get all the "Members" or "Friends" of a particular organisation, and string
all their email addresses into a text file that the user can cut and paste
into the Blind Copy (BCC) part of an outgoing email. In this way it is
possible to "mass mail" all the "Friends" or the "Members" of the society
with a single message. (Actually, it isn't, because most mail servers have a
limit on the number of recipients, so the function below actually breaks the
file into records containing 25 emails; this does not overload the mail
server, but it means the user has to cut and paste into as many mails as
there are rows...

Note that because the length of email addresses can vary, the 25  varying
addresses in each record will cause the record length to vary also.

Of course it would be fairly easy to actually pop up the outgoing mail form
with the BCC filled in, using CDOSYS but, after discussion with the users
they decided they'd rather just have the list somewhere handy so they can do
this whenever they have time. The answer seemed to be to put it on the
desktop and that's when everything turned to custard for me...

Anyway, it is resolved now, works like a dream, and everybody is happy.
Here's the COBOL...

 identification division.
 program-id.     'BCCLIST'.
 installation.
 author.        Peter E. C. Dashwood.
 date-written.   Oct 2004.
*
*remarks.
*
*      Function to Create Blind Copy lists of email addresses for
*             bulk mailing Friends and Members in MEDEA.
*
*
*
*    INPUT Parameters:
*
*
*    1. An Action Flag.
*
*       Indicates whether to Create for Members only, or for all "Friends"
*       of the society.
*
*
*    OUTPUT from the module:
*
*    1. A line sequential file with records containing 25 recipients
*       email addresses separated by semi-colons, suitable to load into a
mailto:
*
*    Remember to comment out Linkage section and swap
*    Procedure Division headers when debugging as a standalone program...
*
*    *******************************************************
 environment division.
 configuration section.
 source-computer. IBM-PC.
 object-computer. IBM-PC.
 special-names.
 input-output section.
 file-control.
     select BCCList-FILE assign OUTFILE-NAME
                 organization is line sequential
                 access mode is sequential.

*------------------------  DATA DIVISION  ---------------------
 data division.
 file section.
 fd   BCCList-FILE
                  RECORD IS VARYING IN SIZE FROM 1 TO 32000
                  DEPENDING ON OUTPUT-LEN
                  LABEL RECORDS STANDARD
                  data record is BCCList-RECORD.
 01   BCCList-RECORD     pic x(32000).


 working-storage section.
 01  end-flag                    pic x.
     88 not-finished             value zero.
     88 finished                 value '1'.


 01  arithmetic-fields usage comp-5.
     12 OUTPUT-LEN        pic 9(7).
     12 OUT-POS    pic s9(7).
     12 LEN   pic s9(7).

 01  name-work  pic x(30).
 01  value-work  pic x(100).
 01  OUTFILE-NAME         pic x(255).
 01  filename-work        pic x(20).
 01  out-count            pic s9(4) comp-5.
*
* HOST VARIABLE definitions for this program.
*  (May be obtained from multiple tables)
*
     EXEC SQL BEGIN DECLARE SECTION END-EXEC.
 01  ClientID                 picture s9(9) comp.
 01  MemberFlag               picture x.
 01  email                    picture x(50).
 01  StartDate                picture x(11).
 01  StartTime                picture x(5).
 01  Statusz                  picture x(1).
 01  LastAction               picture x(4).
 01  Timestampz               picture x(25).
* Other Host Variables...
 01  DBCount      PIC S9(7).
 01  SQLSTATE      PIC X(5).
 01  SQLMSG        PIC X(128).
     EXEC SQL END DECLARE SECTION END-EXEC.

 01  subscripts usage comp-5.
    12 J      pic s9(4).
    12 K      pic s9(4).
    12 L      pic s9(4).

 01  CSIDL_DESKTOP           pic s9(7) comp-5 value zero.
 01  ITEMIDLIST              pic x(5).
 01  DesktopPath             pic x(512) value spaces.
 01  NULLValue               pic x value low-values.
 01  response-code           pic xx.
*
*
 linkage section.
*
 01  BCCActionFlag  pic x.
     88 extracting-friends value '1'.
     88 extracting-members value '2'.
*     88 BCC-OK                    value '3'.
* Only check for failure...if it succeeds, the original setting remains
intact.
     88 BCC-failed                value '4'.
*

/
 procedure division using BCCActionFlag.
* PROCEDURE DIVISION.
 main section.
 main-start.
*
*  for debugging...
*
*     set extracting-friends to TRUE
*
*  end debugging...
*
    perform a-initialise
    perform b-processing until finished
    perform c-close-down
    .
 main-exit.
    exit program.
*-----------------------------------------------------------------
 a-initialise section.
 a000.
     call "SHGetSpecialFolderLocation"  with STDCALL LINKAGE
            using
              by value 0
              by content CSIDL_DESKTOP      *> Locate the User's desktop
              by reference ITEMIDLIST
            returning  response-code
     end-call
     if response-code NOT = zero *> non-zero response is good...
        call   "SHGetPathFromIDList"      with STDCALL LINKAGE
            using
              by reference ITEMIDLIST
                           DesktopPath
            returning response-code
        end-call
        if response-code = zero *> zero response is bad...
           move low-values to DesktopPath (1:1)
        else
           move DesktopPath to OUTFILE-NAME
        end-if
     else
        move low-values to DesktopPath (1:1)
     end-if
*
* If the above API calls succeeded, the DesktopPath field will contain an
* ASCIIZ string pointing to the desktop, otherwise it will be a single
* NULL (empty ASCIIZ string).  If this happens the output BCC list of
* email addresses will be dropped in the root directory of the system.
*
     set not-finished to  TRUE
     if extracting-members
        string
           OUTFILE-NAME
              delimited by NULLValue
           '\BCCMembers.txt'
              delimited by size
                into OUTFILE-NAME
        end-string
     else
        string
           OUTFILE-NAME
              delimited by NULLValue
           '\BCCFriends.txt'
              delimited by size
                into OUTFILE-NAME
        end-string
     end-if
     open output BCCList-FILE
*
*   Define SQL cursor...
*
    EXEC SQL
       declare BCCCUR01 cursor for
       SELECT ClientID,
              MemberFlag,
              email
        FROM   Client
        ORDER BY email
    END-EXEC
    EXEC SQL
       OPEN BCCCUR01
    END-EXEC
*    display 'cursor opened. SQLSTATE=' SQLSTATE
    if SQLSTATE NOT = '00000'
       set finished to TRUE
        set BCC-failed to TRUE
<remaining code snipped>

Hope this is of help to somebody...

Pete.
```

## Thread: OT: — 1 msgs

### OT:

- **From:** Warren Simmons <wsimmons5@optonline.net>
- **Date:** 2004-10-24T14:46:27+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7fPed.65956$YM4.22643271@news4.srv.hcvlny.cv.net>`

```
The following is a posting on Freshmeat today, Sunday. There is another
posting with it about the program itself. I wondered if this tool
would work in cobol, and if so, how.

wsimmons5@optonline.net


phpCodeGenie 1.0 (Documentation)

phpCodeGenie 3.0.2 (Default)
	Section: Unix

	  	

Added: Fri, Jan 24th 2003 08:48 PDT (1 year, 9 months ago) 	Updated: 
Sat, Oct 23rd 2004 09:22 PDT (yesterday)


Screenshot About:
phpCodeGenie (PCG) is a code generator for database driven applications. 
PCG can talk to different databases and generate entire applications for 
you. While the core version generates mostly PHP code, phpCodeGenie can 
be modified via user plugins to generate code in any programming 
language. It will build data entry forms, insert scripts, database 
lister scripts, edit record forms, update record scripts, delete 
confirmation scripts, delete scripts, search forms, search scripts, and 
other frontend/database interaction code. Basically, it provides a basic 
code base which you can build on.

Release focus: Minor security fixes
```

## Thread: Server Express Animator/Oracle Issues — 17 msgs

_Topics: [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### Server Express Animator/Oracle Issues

- **From:** ctaliercio@yahoo.com (Chris)
- **Date:** 2004-10-25T06:54:32-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8cc07162.0410250554.5abe090@posting.google.com>`

```
Hello all. I'm hoping someone else has experienced this problem and
can offer some assistance. I am in the process of upgrading from MF OC
4.2 to SE 2.2 SP1. My environment is HP-UX 11i, Oracle 9.2.0.4.

My problem is an unusual one. I have rebuilt the rtsora using the
$ORACLE_HOME/precomp/lib/ins_precomp.mk file. The runtime unit appears
to be functioning properly.

My next step was to start rebuilding my application. Previously we had
been using separate procob18 and cob commands to compile the programs,
however now I am using the following command line to compile:

cob -ug -a -v \
        -k ${FILENAME}.pco \
        -C"p(cobsql) cobsqltype==oracle end-cobsql endp" \
        -C IBMCOMP \
        -C NESTCALL

I have program A, which prompts for a user id/password then call
program B to connect to the database. After a successful connection,
control is returned to program A which then procedes with whatever
else the application requires.

Here is where things get odd: when I run these programs using the new
rtsora, everything is is great - the application runs exactly as it
has always run using OC 4.2. Now, when I try to animate the
application (using 'export COBSW=+1' and then 'rtsora progA') I get an
error code 12540 from Oracle when the connection is attempted.
According to Oracle, this error means "TNS: internal limit restriction
exceeded", with a cause of "Too many TNS connections open
simultaneously" and an action of "Wait for connections to close and
re-try." I am the only one using the test server where I am
experimenting with the upgrade process - so this doesn't make any
sense.

To make things even more bizarre, when I directly animate progB and
force the user id/passwd information into the appropriate variables,
the connection to Oracle succeeds.

I'm curious as to why:

1)Why would the results under animator be any different than when I am
running the application normally?

2) Why would the connection work directly, but not when inside of a
"called" program?

Sorry to be so long-winded, but I figured the more detail the better
in this scenario.

Any and all help is appreciated.

Thanks,
Chris
```

#### ↳ Re: Server Express Animator/Oracle Issues

- **From:** "Simon Tobias" <Simon.Tobias@nospam.microfocus.com>
- **Date:** 2004-10-25T15:51:26
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<clj318$qh1$1@hyperion.microfocus.com>`
- **References:** `<8cc07162.0410250554.5abe090@posting.google.com>`

```
Hi Chris.

Not sure whether it's relevant or not, but you should be passing
cobsqltype==oracle8 rather than cobsqltype==oracle if you're using Oracle 8
or later. In addition, as the Oracle-provided makefile links using the
threaded run-time, you should pass -t to cob when building your application.

When debugging the app, are the contents of the host variables used for your
CONNECT statement being populated correctly? Are those host variables
defined as CHAR or VARCHAR? If the latter, then you will need to ensure that
the length-element of the host variable is set correctly, else it's possible
that trailing spaces will be passed through to Oracle (though I would expect
a different error then).

Have you contacted Micro Focus SupportLine about this, either directly, or
via the COBOL Portal forum accessible from http://www.cobolportal.com ?

SimonT.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Server Express Animator/Oracle Issues

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** Wiggy <wignomore@btopenworld.com>
- **Date:** 2004-10-26T05:48:22+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<clkof5$fca$1@hercules.btinternet.com>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<psirn01kkvmdjrdbi5kvbl3q6fusb8uh4m@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<8cc07162.0410250554.5abe090@posting.google.com> <clj318$qh1$1@hyperion.microfocus.com> <8cc07162.0410251050.7ddb543b@posting.google.com> <cljrc4$ln8$1@titan.btinternet.com> <8cc07162.0410251850.63afd8a1@posting.google.com> <psirn01kkvmdjrdbi5kvbl3q6fusb8uh4m@4ax.com>`

```

> Micro Focus thinks so. They recommend executable/so as the norm and
> say they support gnt only for backward compatibility. 

Not true. Micro Focus fully supports use of int/gnt/so/exe for 
deployment of applications.

See Chapter 3 (Packaging Applications) of the Server Express User's 
Guide for further information on the various executable types supported, 
including pro's and con's. You can access the docs from 
http://supportline.microfocus.com/ .

Robert, I'd be interesting in knowing where it had been suggested that 
gnt was only provided for backwards compatibility (off-list if you'd 
prefer).

SimonT.
```

```
<wignomore@btopenworld.com> wrote:

…[12 more quoted lines elided]…

For instance, chapter 2 of Server Express/Migration/Library (2.2)
says:

"In this product, we recommend that you package your application using
native UNIX system executable types."

And chapter 2 of Net Express/Migration says:

"Package it as .dll and .exe files. This is very easy using the
application's project, as the default options for projects cause it to
be done this way. 

Alternatively, you could write a trigger program yourself, calling the
main .int or .gnt file explicitly. You would link just the trigger
program as a .exe file. "
```

_(thread depth: 8)_

- **From:** "Simon Tobias" <Simon.Tobias@nospam.microfocus.com>

```
None of what you pasted suggests (to me) that gnt code is only supported for
compatibility purposes.

The doc I referred to goes into the pro's and con's of the various
executable formats, and the recommendations you found reflect what Micro
Focus believes would be most efficient, though the choice is with the
customer. Which isn't quite the same as ".. they support gnt only for
backward compatibility.".

Certainly the Library utility is documented as such, but that's not the same
as gnt code.

SimonT.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Server Express Animator/Oracle Issues

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** Wiggy <wignomore@btopenworld.com>
- **Date:** 2004-10-26T05:58:55+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<clkp2v$ftp$1@hercules.btinternet.com>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<8cc07162.0410251850.63afd8a1@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<8cc07162.0410250554.5abe090@posting.google.com> <clj318$qh1$1@hyperion.microfocus.com> <8cc07162.0410251050.7ddb543b@posting.google.com> <cljrc4$ln8$1@titan.btinternet.com> <8cc07162.0410251850.63afd8a1@posting.google.com>`

```
> I even looked into not using an rtsora, but creating a Shared Library
> of Oracle DBMS Routines (Section 9.2.2 in the Database Access
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> file not found in drive/directory".

The only change to the cob command-line specified within the Oracle 
makefile should be substituting -xe for -ze, in order to create the 
shared object.You may want to check that the cob command-line is only 
referenced once within the makefile though, as I've seen cases on 
certain platforms where the cmdline was referenced twice.

Assuming you have created, say, myoralib.sl , then you would need to 
compile your app along the lines of  :

cob -itk mycobapp.pco -C "initcall(myoralib.sl) p(cobsql) ...".

You may need to ensure that the shared object has 555 permissions.

Specifically on HP-UX, in order to utilise this method, due to an 
apparent Oracle bug, you need to load the following OS patch from HP 
(note that this has most likely been superceded by a later patch) :

HP-UX 11.0 : PHSS_26262
HP-UX 11.11 : PHSS_26263

Having installed the appropriate patch, you then need to set the 
LD_PRELOAD environment variable to point to the Oracle client-side 
library, e.g.

LD_PRELOAD=$ORACLE_HOME/lib32/libclntsh.sl
export LD_PRELOAD

> Do you think that there are any advantages to the shared library
> method over the rtsora method? I'd rather put all of my efforts into
> pursuing the issue that will be most fruitful in the end.

If you intend to call your COBOL Oracle app from Java, you would need to 
use the shared library method. Likewise if you chose in future to deploy 
under Enterprise Server.

If you can email me a demo off-list, I can check this out.

SimonT.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Server Express Animator/Oracle Issues

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** "Simon Tobias" <Simon.Tobias@nospam.microfocus.com>
- **Date:** 2004-10-26T16:46:05
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<cllqkf$52k$1@hyperion.microfocus.com>`
- **References:** `<8cc07162.0410250554.5abe090@posting.google.com> <clj318$qh1$1@hyperion.microfocus.com> <8cc07162.0410251050.7ddb543b@posting.google.com> <cljrc4$ln8$1@titan.btinternet.com> <8cc07162.0410251850.63afd8a1@posting.google.com> <clkp2v$ftp$1@hercules.btinternet.com> <8cc07162.0410260711.79576c9c@posting.google.com>`

```
> OK Simon - using your tips and tricks I was able to get the system
> going using the Shared Library instead of the rtsora. Its a good thing
> Micro Focus has a guy like you monitoring the user group to fill in
> the gaping holes in their documentation - that would not have been
> something readily apparent to me.

The addition of LD_PRELOAD functionality was something we stumbled across
while investigating getting Java apps to be able to call Cobsql apps on
HP-UX. From memory, the need for pre-loading the Oracle client was due to
one or more of the Oracle-provided shared objects containing unresolved
symbols. It's more of an Oracle issue, though I will see if we can get
something added to our Knowledge Base (available from
http://supportline.microfocus.com ) to cover this issue, as you're not the
first person to encounter it.

> Unfortunately I am still having the same problem while animating. I
> would be happy to send you the .pco files that I am attempting to work
> with, if that would help - I'm just afraid that setting them up and
> getting them to work on your system may not be so simple. This code is
> somewhat convoluted (I inherited it from its author).

I guess if you could send your sources to me, I can try and reproduce the
issue in a cut-down.

I've since been in contact with the SupportLine person who you'd been in
touch with, so will give them the updated status.

Regards,
SimonT.
```

#### ↳ Re: Server Express Animator/Oracle Issues

- **From:** Robert Wagner <robert@wagner.net.yourmammaharvests>
- **Date:** 2004-10-25T16:24:54+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<uk9qn0l9nsrg2sc73v3lbl6dra4qo4m5tf@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<8cc07162.0410250554.5abe090@posting.google.com>`

```
On 25 Oct 2004 06:54:32 -0700, ctaliercio@yahoo.com (Chris) wrote:

>Here is where things get odd: when I run these programs using the new
>rtsora, everything is is great - the application runs exactly as it
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
>sense.

Sounds like you've hit a process limit on the server. Ask your DBA to
edit $ORACLE_HOME/dbs/init.ora and look for keyword 'processes'. If
any are small numbers, make them bigger. Also look for 'tns' and small
cache sizes. After changing, bounce the database.
```

#### ↳ Re: Server Express Animator/Oracle Issues

- **From:** ctaliercio@yahoo.com (Chris)
- **Date:** 2004-10-26T12:45:48-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8cc07162.0410261145.23362fed@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<8cc07162.0410250554.5abe090@posting.google.com>`

```
ctaliercio@yahoo.com (Chris) wrote in message news:<8cc07162.0410250554.5abe090@posting.google.com>...
> Hello all. I'm hoping someone else has experienced this problem and
> can offer some assistance. I am in the process of upgrading from MF OC
…[51 more quoted lines elided]…
> Chris

ALL:


Thanks for all of the input and suggestions.

After a LOT of investigative work and debugging, I have located the
problem: it appears that program A makes a few calls to "CBL_"
routines before it reaches the call to program B. I found that if I
skip over these calls, and go directly to the call to program B I do
not have the connection issue any longer.

Apparently, when upgrading from the current 32-bit environment to the
64-bit environment, there is some work to be done when it comes to
parameter passing - especially when it involves "canned" or "system"
routines.

So - I think once I straighten out the parameter calls to these
routines I should be OK.

Thanks again for all of the help.


Chris
```

---

```
I can't find an applicable filter in my newsgroup, but I don't get anything from
Robert Wagner <robert@wagner.net.yourmammaharvests>.

I see a lot of quotes as his posts tend to get replies.

I'm wondering if there is a server side filter that sees "yourmamma" or
something.

I hope I'm not filtering out more useful messages without knowing about them.
```

#### ↳ Re: Missing Wagner

- **From:** mwojcik@newsguy.com (Michael Wojcik)
- **Date:** 2004-10-26T16:34:58+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<cllubi0i7l@news4.newsguy.com>`
- **References:** `<clj4p2$hmt$1@peabody.colorado.edu>`

```

In article <clj4p2$hmt$1@peabody.colorado.edu>, "Howard Brazee" <howard@brazee.net> writes:
> I can't find an applicable filter in my newsgroup, but I don't get anything from
> Robert Wagner <robert@wagner.net.yourmammaharvests>.
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> something.

Possible, though that seems like a fairly tame expression for a
content filter to reject.  I doubt you're sitting behind a filter
that rejects invalid poster email addresses, or you'd hardly see
anything.

I suspect it's more likely that some node in your downstream path
doesn't like Robert's messages for some other reason, possibly
because his NNTP posting host (69.115.155.63) uses dynamically-
assigned DNS records.  (It currently has working A and PTR records,
which I'd think would be enough, but maybe there's a host on the
route that wants the NNTP-Posting-Host header to have a FQDN rather
than a dotted-decimal address.  Also, I notice that the current PTR
record for that address doesn't match the name in the X-Trace
header - some hop may not like that.)

Hmm... I checked the Path for one of Robert's messages, and it
contains "167.206.3.103.MISMATCH" as one of the hops.  It's been a
while since I poked around in the NNTP specs, so I couldn't say
offhand what that "MISMATCH" means, but it does look suspect.  Maybe
a hop along your feed has a heuristic that rejects messages with
"MISMATCH" hops as possible forgeries.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Missing Wagner

- **From:** Robert Wagner <nospam-robert@wagner.net>
- **Date:** 2004-10-26T18:42:01+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<s63tn0db8atk7hqedm2bvn9tlai57fvl7j@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<clj4p2$hmt$1@peabody.colorado.edu> <cllubi0i7l@news4.newsguy.com>`

```
On 26 Oct 2004 16:34:58 GMT, mwojcik@newsguy.com (Michael Wojcik)
wrote:


>I suspect it's more likely that some node in your downstream path
>doesn't like Robert's messages for some other reason, possibly
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
>"MISMATCH" hops as possible forgeries.

news3-ge1.srv.hcvlny.cv.net [167.206.3.103] appears to be the output
portal of posting host news4.srv.hcvlny.cv.net [69.115.155.63].

Incoming messages pass through news3.optonline.net, which is an alias
for news3.srv.hcvlny.cv.net [167.206.3.102]. Their final destination
is news4.

CV is Cablevision aka Optonline (NY/NJ). It is possible they are
blacklisted because they don't authenticate users. Anyone can post
through them.

I changed my email on this posting.

The problem will end Friday, when I move out of Cablevision
territory.Two weeks hence I'll be posting through Earthlink/Time
Warner Greensboro.
```

```
Good research. It would be practical for a mailserver/forwarder to
validate node names via DNS; it would be impractical to look them up
on registrars (too slow). Moreover, if they don't have a DNS entry,
they are unusable .. today.
```

```
is expected to last 2 ï¿½ 4 weeks.

Job City: New York City 
Job State: NY
Job Country: US
Salary: DOE

format. Do not send e-mail attachments.
```

## Thread: Help! Converting files from a Cobol app — 12 msgs

_Topics: [`Migration and conversion`](topics.md#migration) · [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help! Converting files from a Cobol app

- **From:** "Vilco" <a@b.c>
- **Date:** 2004-10-26T08:41:06+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<C4ofd.1494$Es2.35217@twister2.libero.it>`

```
I'm new to this group, so... hello everybody!
I'm going to move data from an old application written in Cobol
my win32 application. To do this, I should export the data files
to plain text format. They're indexed files, the data files have
no extnsion and the index files have IDX extension. The
application is in Cobol because, if I try to run it in a wrong
path, I get the error "Cobol runtime not installed", and moreover
there is the COB directory & stuff.

My first difficulty is to determine the exact format of these
archives. I've found the EXTFH.EXE application, which I found to
be a kind of library used by cobol to access different kinds of
archives, but I can not figure out which is used in this
application. There is also a REBUILD.EXE utility which I found
confusing infos about: it should be a file maintenance utility of
some sort.

How can I determine the exact format of these files? And,
moreover, where can I find an utility to convert them into plain
ASCII format?

Thanks in advance
    Vilco
```

#### ↳ Re: Help! Converting files from a Cobol app

- **From:** "Vilco" <a@b.c>
- **Date:** 2004-10-26T09:14:28+00:00

```
BTW, I copy here the BAT which launches the Cobol application,
maybe this makes the file version easier to determine:

SET COBDIR=..\COB
SET TEMP=..\TEMP
SET EXTFHBUL=65535
SET IDXDATBUF=65535
SET COBSW=+M
CLS
..\COB\GESTMENU (-F-N+1+L+B+B1)
APP
PAUSE
CLS

It looks like this is a Microsoft - Micro Focus Cobol
application.
Here's what it says when I try to run the REBUILD utility:
Microsoft (R) COBOL file management utility version 5.0
COBOL software by Micro Focus
Copyright Microsoft ...
Copyright Micro Focus...

I hope this helps.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Help! Converting files from a Cobol app

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2004-10-26T17:54:09+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<5bwfd.30558$nl.10946@pd7tw3no>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<Uzofd.1414$Ni.42525@twister1.libero.it>`
- **References:** `<C4ofd.1494$Es2.35217@twister2.libero.it> <Uzofd.1414$Ni.42525@twister1.libero.it>`

```
Vilco wrote:

>BTW, I copy here the BAT which launches the Cobol application,
>maybe this makes the file version easier to determine:
…[22 more quoted lines elided]…
>
Your programs were compiled using Microsoft COBOL V 5.0 which is a 
re-badged Micro Focus  Version 3+ (the old DOS version)  -  formats  are 
identical. Your filename (no extension) is the data file and the 
filename.idx is an index containing your PrimeKeys and perhaps Alternate 
Keys.

Do you only have the compiled programs - in which case you are somewhat 
stuck. You need the SOURCE of the M/S programs that contain the file 
layouts. For example a record layout could be something like following :-

01 MyRecord.
 05 PrimeKey      pic x(06) or pic 9(06).
 05 Name            pic x(30).
 05 Value1           pic 9(03)v9(02) comp-3.
 05 Value2           pic s9(06) comp-3.   ------ signed value (minus if 
negative)

Without the format it is difficult to establish where fields start and 
finish and how numerics are stored, could be 'packed' as above or just 
plain 9(03)v9(02) and s9(06). (The 'v' is an implied decimal point).

Given you have the formats - then you need to write programs reading the 
above ISAM (Indexed Sequential Access Mode) files, sequentially.

01 MyTextRecord.
 05 T-PrimeKey      pic x(06) or pic 9(06).
 05 pic x value ",".
 05 T-Name            pic x(30).
 05 pic x value ",".
 05 T-Value1           pic 999.99.
 05 pic x value ",".
 05 T-Value2           pic -999999.   ------ signed value (minus if 
negative)
 05 pic x value ",".

Open input MyfileName
Open output MyTextFile
set fileNotFinis to true
perform until fileFinis
   read MyFilename next record
        at end set FileFinis to true
        not at end
            move PrimeKey to T-PrimeKey
            move Name to  T-Name          
            move Value1 to T-Value1        
            move Value2 to T-Value2        
            write MyTextRecord
    end-read

end-perform

Close MyfileName
Close MyTextFile

But you need those record formats and either a Microsoft or Micro Focus 
compiler to access the existing files.  (The alternative go googling as 
suggested in other messages, looking for support on this topic in this 
Newsgroup).

HTH

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

#### ↳ Re: Help! Converting files from a Cobol app

- **From:** Frederico Fonseca <real-email-in-msg-spam@email.com>
- **Date:** 2004-10-26T14:44:00+01:00

```
On Tue, 26 Oct 2004 08:41:06 GMT, "Vilco" <a@b.c> wrote:

>I'm new to this group, so... hello everybody!
>I'm going to move data from an old application written in Cobol
…[17 more quoted lines elided]…
>ASCII format?

If you have the source of the Selectï¿½s and File Descriptions then you
can do/ask someone to do it for you, a small program that will extract
te information from the files and write this as a text file, with or
without field separators, and with all numeric fields converted to
usage display.

You can also search this group (www.deja.com) for similar threads and
see what has been said about this subject.
This has happend a lot, and there are links to some sites that sell
commercial products to help you do this if you donï¿½t have the sources
mentioned above.




Frederico Fonseca
ema il: frederico_fonseca at syssoft-int.com
```

#### ↳ Re: Help! Converting files from a Cobol app

- **From:** Robert Wagner <robert@wagner.net.yourmammaharvests>
- **Date:** 2004-10-26T14:50:49+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<jnksn09fmqrpkq5bqjv5ubcs8ls4ohe265@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<C4ofd.1494$Es2.35217@twister2.libero.it>`

```
On Tue, 26 Oct 2004 08:41:06 GMT, "Vilco" <a@b.c> wrote:

>I'm new to this group, so... hello everybody!
>I'm going to move data from an old application written in Cobol
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>ASCII format?

Look for dfed (data file editor) and dfconv (data file converter).
Also look for source code and copybooks, which will have extension
cob, cbl or cpy.

If there are no packed or binary fields, conversion is a snap. If
there are, you'll have to expand those fields to display-format. 

If you have copybooks, I posted a program that will use them to
convert the files to comma-delimited.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Help! Converting files from a Cobol app

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2004-10-26T22:37:14+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ukAfd.38596$Pl.22357@pd7tw1no>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<Avzfd.90311$b5.4361003@news3.tin.it>`
- **References:** `<C4ofd.1494$Es2.35217@twister2.libero.it> <jnksn09fmqrpkq5bqjv5ubcs8ls4ohe265@4ax.com> <9JawBIjeflB@jpberlin-l.willms.jpberlin.de> <Avzfd.90311$b5.4361003@news3.tin.it>`

```
Vilco wrote:

>Lueko Willms wrote:
>  
…[44 more quoted lines elided]…
>

If you haven't got MS or MF manuals - use the following link to the 
current Net Express V 4 on-line books :-

http://supportline.microfocus.com/supportline/documentation/books/nx40/nx40indx.htm

Not necessarily so, but those packed numeric values are in all 
likelihood specified as variations of the format of comp-3.  Have a look 
at the Language Reference Manual for comp-3 - 2.6.4.4. and 8.26.

I'm not sure, but from what you have written with regard to the text 
fields - it's possible that the originator designed the files with data 
compression. Again,  look at the book on File Handling, Section 6.3.

Without question, if you can get copies of the file format sources from 
the original developer - that's far the easiest route to go. For a few 
bucks they might even be prepared to write you the programs putting out 
the data as comma delimited text files. So the 'few bucks' might be 
$200-500 - still probably cheaper than your time figuring it out.

Jimmy
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Help! Converting files from a Cobol app

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** Robert Wagner <spamblocker-robert@wagner.net>
- **Date:** 2004-10-27T03:16:41+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<tn4un0lsqubqb7p3ddvma93rvks9c4ilml@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<C4ofd.1494$Es2.35217@twister2.libero.it> <jnksn09fmqrpkq5bqjv5ubcs8ls4ohe265@4ax.com> <9JawBIjeflB@jpberlin-l.willms.jpberlin.de> <Avzfd.90311$b5.4361003@news3.tin.it>`

```
On Tue, 26 Oct 2004 21:40:48 GMT, "Vilco" <t@h.c> wrote:

>The character fields, like the surname and name of the persons,
>are not full-plain text: every some six-to-ten characters there is one inconsistent character, like a german umlaut in the half of
>an otherwise plain italian surname (like "ferraï¿½i" instead of "ferrari").

The file system was extfh and DATACOMPRESS was turned on. It uses two
similar RLL algorithms, which compress strings of repeating
characters. One has two bytes, the first being binary 2-31 and the
second, the character. The other has three bytes, the first being DC4
(ASCII 20, the character shown above), the second is binary 2-255 and
the third, the character. If you read the file via extfh, you don't
need to worry about expansion.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Help! Converting files from a Cobol app

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "Vilco" <a@b.c>
- **Date:** 2004-10-27T13:10:59+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<D7Nfd.3470$Ni.102658@twister1.libero.it>`
- **References:** `<C4ofd.1494$Es2.35217@twister2.libero.it> <jnksn09fmqrpkq5bqjv5ubcs8ls4ohe265@4ax.com> <9JawBIjeflB@jpberlin-l.willms.jpberlin.de> <Avzfd.90311$b5.4361003@news3.tin.it> <tn4un0lsqubqb7p3ddvma93rvks9c4ilml@4ax.com>`

```
Robert Wagner wrote:

> The file system was extfh and DATACOMPRESS was turned on.
> It uses two similar RLL algorithms, which compress
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> extfh, you don't need to worry about expansion.

I looked around the web and I found tons of pages talking
about EXTFH.CFG, but this file is not present in the app's
folders. Is this a problem?
Moreover, is this ExtFH.EXE callable only from Cobol apps?
Because I have never used Cobol and I do not have any cobol
related tool.

Regarding your previous post, where you wrote:

> Look for dfed (data file editor) and dfconv (data file
> converter). Also look for source code and copybooks,
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> them to convert the files to comma-delimited

I checked the app's folders and found:
- no dfed / dfconv files
- cpy files: none
- cbl files: just one (and 2 empty, as well). One seems to
  reorganize data in one table (the main archive) and also
  specifies the fields type and length of that archive.
  But there are many archives in the app, not just that one.
- cob files: 6 under the COB folder, 1 under the FILES folder,
  where the data files are stored. They look to be compiled,
  and contain many non alphanumeric characters along with
  many user-interface strings (like the user defined error
  messages).

So, how can I read these data files to convert them to plain
text?
Ah, last but not least: if it was still unclear for someone,
I'm a total newbie in cobol & stuff :(
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ DONE ! Thanks everybody :)

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** "Vilco" <a@b.c>
- **Date:** 2004-10-27T14:21:39+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<T9Ofd.3591$Ni.104917@twister1.libero.it>`
- **References:** `<C4ofd.1494$Es2.35217@twister2.libero.it> <jnksn09fmqrpkq5bqjv5ubcs8ls4ohe265@4ax.com> <9JawBIjeflB@jpberlin-l.willms.jpberlin.de> <Avzfd.90311$b5.4361003@news3.tin.it> <tn4un0lsqubqb7p3ddvma93rvks9c4ilml@4ax.com> <D7Nfd.3470$Ni.102658@twister1.libero.it>`

```
Messing around with the REBUILD utility, I found out how to
convert the files in C-ISAM plain text format:
REBUILD.EXE ARCHIVENAME. , NEWARCHIVENAME.TXT /s:MF /t:C-ISAM
Compression resolved both on numeric and thext values.

Thanks very much to all who helped :)
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Help! Converting files from a Cobol app

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2004-10-27T13:54:28-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0410271254.7a22cbf8@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<C4ofd.1494$Es2.35217@twister2.libero.it> <jnksn09fmqrpkq5bqjv5ubcs8ls4ohe265@4ax.com> <9JawBIjeflB@jpberlin-l.willms.jpberlin.de> <Avzfd.90311$b5.4361003@news3.tin.it> <tn4un0lsqubqb7p3ddvma93rvks9c4ilml@4ax.com> <D7Nfd.3470$Ni.102658@twister1.libero.it>`

```
"Vilco" <a@b.c> wrote 

> RW> If you read the file via
> RW> extfh, you don't need to worry about expansion.
 
> I looked around the web and I found tons of pages talking
> about EXTFH.CFG, but this file is not present in the app's
> folders. Is this a problem?

No.

> Moreover, is this ExtFH.EXE callable only from Cobol apps?
> Because I have never used Cobol and I do not have any cobol
> related tool.

The MF/MS Cobol development system includes EXTFH.OBJ which can be
linked into a C program and used to access the data files. 
Documentation to do this, and a license allowing this to be done, is
supplied with the product.

Microsoft Cobol 5 is a rebadged version of MicroFocus Cobol 3.0 (not
Server Express 3.0).  If you can find docs for EXTFH for MF Cobol 2.5
- 3.4 then you can see how to use it in C.

> RW> Look for dfed (data file editor) and dfconv (data file
> RW> converter). 

> I checked the app's folders and found:
> - no dfed / dfconv files

No you won't have RW was talking about something completely different.
 In any case, even if those existed in MS Cobol 5 they may not have
been issued with the application.

> RW> If you have copybooks, I posted a program that will use
> RW> them to convert the files to comma-delimited

Robert, that was completely useless to someone without the compiler.

> So, how can I read these data files to convert them to plain
> text?

It seems that you have managed to coerse the REBUILD to convert to
uncompressed. The hard bit now is to identify what the numbers mean
which you will have to do by inference.
```

```
Archive-name: computer-lang/cobol-faq
Posting-Frequency: twice a month
Last-Modified: 2002/05/06
Version: 3.03
URL: http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm
Reply-To: wmklein@ix.netcom.com


                            COBOL FAQ
                  (Frequently Asked Questions)

Last-Modified: Spring 2002

The full COBOL FAQ can be viewed at:

  http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm

If you have comments, corrects, deletions, or additions, please send
private email to:

    wmklein <at> ix.netcom.com

Toward the top of the FAQ is a pointer to the appendix listing the most
recent significant updates and changes.

This FAQ answers such questions as (and addresses topics such as):

 - Where can I get a COBOL compiler? 
 - Is there a free COBOL compiler? 
 - Where can I contact... ?
 - What is happening with the draft of the next COBOL Standard and what
is in it? 
 - What about OO COBOL? 
 - Books about COBOL
 - COBOL Tools
 - What can/should I post in the COBOL newsgroups?
 - What about USAGE? COMP? Storage for data in xyz format? etc?
 - How do I get started with COBOL? Where can I get education?
Tutorials? Etc

and much, MUCH more
```

## Thread: Server Express Animator/Oracle Issues - Again — 6 this month / 9 total

_Topics: [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### Server Express Animator/Oracle Issues - Again

- **From:** ctaliercio@yahoo.com (Chris)
- **Date:** 2004-10-27T14:21:07-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8cc07162.0410271321.1f9cd0c0@posting.google.com>`

```
OK - I thought I had this figured out, but apparently not.

I apologize for what is to be a lengthy post here, but I figure the
best way to get a solution is to provide working sample code to
illustrate the problem I am having.

PLATFORM: HP-UX 11i (11.11)
COBOL: Server Express 2.2 SP1

I have constructed a Shared Library of Oracle SQL functions per Micro
Focus' documentation and named it orasqllib.sl.

I also have the following test program:

       identification division.
       program-id. connectme.

       data division.

       working-storage section.

           exec sql include sqlca end-exec.

           exec sql declare dbname database end-exec.

           exec sql begin declare section end-exec.

       01  db-user                     pic  x(21)  varying.
       01  db-str                      pic  x(10)  varying.

           exec sql end declare section end-exec.


       procedure division.

           display "Hello World"
             at 0101
             with foreground-color 4
           end-display

           move "scott/tiger" to db-user-arr
           move 21 to db-user-len

           move spaces to db-str-arr
           move 0 to db-str-len

           exec sql
             connect
               :db-user
             at
               dbname
             using
               :db-str
           end-exec

           evaluate  sqlcode
               when  0
                   display "Connected"
                     at 0201
                     with foreground-color 4
                   end-display
               when  other
                   display
                     "Error: "
                     sqlerrmc(1:sqlerrml - 1)
                    at 0201
                   end-display
           end-evaluate

           goback returning 0
           .

I compile the program using the following command line:

cob -g -a -v -t \
 -k connectme.pco \
 -C"p(cobsql) cobsqltype==oracle8 end sqlcheck==full
maxopencursors==45 endp" \
 -C IBMCOMP -C NESTCALL -C LINKCOUNT=256 -C INITCALL=orasqllib.sl -C
SERIAL

When I issue the following command: cobrun_t connectme.int

I get the following results:

Attempting Connection
Connected

When I animate with the following command: anim_t connectme.int

I get the following results:

Attempting Connection
Error:
ORA-12540: TNS:internal limit restriction exceeded


However, I only get this error when the DISPLAY statement is executed
prior to the connection. If I skip past the connection, I am able to
make the connection successfully. Apparently ANY call to ADIS
interferes with the SQL connect statement.

This is not good, as you can imagine. I need to prompt (on a nice
pretty screen) for user name/password before attempting a connection
to the database.

Any/all help is greatly appreciated.


Thanks in advance,
Chris
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Server Express Animator/Oracle Issues - Again

- **From:** "Simon Tobias" <Simon.Tobias@nospam.microfocus.com>
- **Date:** 2004-10-28T15:02:58
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<clqtch$mqc$1@hyperion.microfocus.com>`
- **References:** `<8cc07162.0410271321.1f9cd0c0@posting.google.com> <clpfac$k02$1@titan.btinternet.com> <8cc07162.0410280354.878708a@posting.google.com>`

```
Hi Chris.

I don't see that you have a ld and linker tools patch installed -- this is
required to implement LD_PRELOAD. The original patch for HP-UX 11i was
PHSS_26263, though according to HP's site, the latest recommended patch
(which supercedes 26263) is PHSS_30966.

Could you try installing that and retesting please?

(Re: your other issue, please email me directly -- without the nospam -- who
you'd spoken to at Micro Focus, and I'll try to follow that up for you).

SimonT.
```

```
Robert,

I agree with you - its definitely the call to ADIS during animation
that is causing the problems with the embedded SQL connect statement.

Also, while I understand that you can debug without anim, sometimes
its not very practical. In this instance I have inherited a source
library that contains over 1740 programs and over 1630 copy members.
It is very useful to be able to "step" through the code - especially
considering some of the spaghetti in this mix.

Thanks for the help.

Chris
```

```
    This FAQ answers such questions as (and addresses topics such as):

     - Is there a free COBOL compiler? 
     - Where can I contact... ?
     - What about OO COBOL? 
     - Books about COBOL
     - COBOL Tools

     - What about USAGE? COMP? Storage for data in xyz format? etc?
     - How do I get started with COBOL? Where can I get education? 
 		Tutorials? Etc
   
   	 and much, MUCH more
```

<a id="t-427fe21a29"></a>
## Thread: Cobol compiler for cross plattform (WinXP/zOS) development — 2 this month / 6 total

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers) · [`Mainframe, z/OS, JCL, CICS`](topics.md#mainframe)_

### Cobol compiler for cross plattform (WinXP/zOS) development

- **From:** Morten <noemail@please.com>
- **Date:** 2004-10-29T10:46:14+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<41820331@news.broadpark.no>`

```
We develop and test our Cobol application (Cobol/CICS/DB2/Batch) on OS/2 (!)
plattform and run on MVS/OS390. Since OS/2 is "out of fashion", we need to
move to Windows XP environment. Doing so we need to replace the development
environment (Editor/Cobol compiler++).

I've checked the Cobol FAQ and Google and this is what I have found: 

* Micro Focus Net Express/Mainframe Express
Very advanced, very expensive, good support for develop/test on Windows run
on zOS. 

* CA-Realia II Workbench
Similar to Micro Focus. 

* IBM Visual Age for Cobol
Look obsolete, and soon not supported

* IBM Websphere Enterprise Developer
Looks like IBM focusing on integration Java/Cobol, less on a pure
development environment

Fujitsu Cobol and a lot of others have no support for mainframe.

I'm I missing something?

Best regards,
Morten.
```

#### ↳ Re: Cobol compiler for cross plattform (WinXP/zOS) development

- **From:** Colin Campbell <cmcampb@adelphia.net>
- **Date:** 2004-10-29T15:16:42-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<WLednfs-uLTWXB_cRVn-1A@adelphia.com>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<41820331@news.broadpark.no>`
- **References:** `<41820331@news.broadpark.no>`

```
Morten wrote:

>We develop and test our Cobol application (Cobol/CICS/DB2/Batch) on OS/2 (!)
>plattform and run on MVS/OS390. Since OS/2 is "out of fashion", we need to
…[26 more quoted lines elided]…
>
Our shop used MicroFocus COBOL many years ago (also on OS/2), and more 
recently, VisualAge COBOL (on both OS/2 and Windows NT).

I liked VisualAge COBOL, because it was pretty clearly a port of IBM's 
mainframe COBOL compiler to run in the PC environments.  Programs which 
were written for the mainframe were easily converted, compiled, and 
executed on the PC.  Programs written on the PC were easy to move to the 
mainframe, too.  Usually, the ASSIGN clause of the SELECT statement was 
all I would have to change in either direction.

I took a look at the IBM COBOL situation, though, and it does appear, as 
you say,  that VisualAge COBOL is being more or less adandoned.  The 
last release was in 2000, and that announcement letter is no longer 
available on the Web.

I expect you can depend on MicroFocus to continue to support mainframe 
development on the PC platform, but I'm not at all current on that set 
of tools.

I would also suggest that your shop might want to revisit its strategy 
of doing development and testing on the PC platform.  This strategy 
seemed to be "all the rage" in the mid-1990's, but IBM has developed 
some very good mainframe tools which might make your platform choice 
different if you looked today.  IBM Debug Tool is one example.  Other 
vendors have highly refined and useful products, too, such as 
configuration managers, file utilities, etc.

The shop where I worked did develop a Windows based version of some 
personnel software in 2001, and used some programs that had been written 
for the mainframe as part of that.  VA COBOL was the tool.  However, to 
a great degree, it is still a mainframe shop, and development and 
testing are done on a separate "test" z/OS image, then compiled and 
moved using a configuration manager (CA-Endevor) to production.
```

<a id="t-d5fd6b6502"></a>
## Thread: IBM's CCCA product — 2 this month / 4 total

### IBM's CCCA product

- **From:** "TammyBain" <baint@cityofrochester.gov>
- **Date:** 2004-10-29T09:34:45-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<64b4dff706cc00dcb57a0a82ed5ab625@localhost.talkaboutprogramming.com>`

```
We are considering purchasing IBM's CCCA software package to help us
convert over 1000 programs from COBOL 74 to COBOL/VSE.  I'm looking for
other organizations that have used this product to learn more about their
experience (good/bad).
```

#### ↳ Re: IBM's CCCA product

- **From:** Colin Campbell <cmcampb@adelphia.net>
- **Date:** 2004-10-29T14:53:25-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<PMydnZWxWOJLJh_cRVn-tg@adelphia.com>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<64b4dff706cc00dcb57a0a82ed5ab625@localhost.talkaboutprogramming.com>`
- **References:** `<64b4dff706cc00dcb57a0a82ed5ab625@localhost.talkaboutprogramming.com>`

```
TammyBain wrote:

>We are considering purchasing IBM's CCCA software package to help us
>convert over 1000 programs from COBOL 74 to COBOL/VSE.  I'm looking for
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>
I have used the tool as part of  VisualAge COBOL (running it on both 
Windows and OS/2), and more recently on OS/390.  It works acceptably, in 
my opinion.  Our migration has been from OS/VS COBOL to Enterprise COBOL 
for z/OS and OS/390.

For OS/390 and z/OS, there are additional choices.  Prince Software 
markets a conversion aid named

The great majority of our programs (around 95%) did not need conversion, 
because we have had standards in place (and a standards checking program 
to enforce them) for many years.  Obsolete verbs like EXHIBIT, EXAMINE, 
and ON have been prohibited.  If your programs still use them, however, 
CCCA will translate them to 1985 ANSI COBOL verbs that give the same 
results.  Many of the programs (say, 3/4 of that 5%) which needed a 
little tweak or two were simply converted manually.  You might consider 
starting by compiling your "old COBOL" programs with your "new COBOL" 
compiler, and see what falls out.  With luck, you may not need to use 
CCCA very often.

At my mainframe shop, we ordered IBM Debug Tool with the optional 
Utilities and Advanced Functions module.  It cost about 1/10 as much as 
the debugger we were using previously (which I don't want to name), and 
one of the utilities was CCCA.  It was my impression that the entire 
debugging package cost less than ordering CCCA separately.  Debug Tool 
can be used with COBOL, as well as other languages, such C/C++ and 
PL/1.  It even does Assembler to a degree these days.  So if your shop 
isn't 100% COBOL, it may be of  great value to you for those "other" 
languages.

It appears that the Debug Tool is available for VSE as well.  I am a big 
fan of this debugger, and if you do not currently have an interactive 
debugger, I would recommend you take a look at it.  I Googled on "IBM 
Debug Tool VSE" and it looks as if the advanced functions stuff can be 
ordered, but I didn't see the "utilities" part mentioned.  You can 
always ask your IBM rep about that, or search IBM.com.

Good luck!
```

<a id="t-4e5f66a214"></a>
## Thread: Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits — 21 this month / 1132 total

### Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits

- **From:** "Chuck Stevens" <charles.stevens@unisys.com>
- **Date:** 2004-10-29T12:01:03-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<clu41g$1t2b$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com>`

```
For those who have been following the "perform forever" and "exit perform
[cycle]" threads, I'm just tidying up the first drafts of two proposals that
I hope to submit Real Soon Now to INCITS/J4 for evaluation and consideration
for a future standard, if nothing else so that they can be added to the
"candidates list" for future consideration.

One of these proposals relaxes the current restriction that an EXIT PERFORM
or EXIT PERFORM CYCLE statement may only appear within a PERFORM ...
END-PERFORM range.

The other proposal is for PERFORM ... UNTIL FALSE.   I didn't want to add a
new reserved word and for that reason rejected PERFORM ... FOREVER.  I was
concerned about the potential semantic ambiguities that one might encounter
in sequences like "PERFORM ... PERFORM ... UNTIL EXIT PERFORM ...
END-PERFORM ...", which led me away from PERFORM ... UNTIL EXIT.  I don't
care for PERFORM ... WITH NO TEST because it really doesn't convey the sense
of iteration.  I am not wedded to the syntax I chose, but I think the
technical reasons for avoiding the other three here presented are sound.

Unless WG4 so directs, these capabilities can't be included in the proposed
2008 standard.  But I can envision no technical barrier at all to the second
of these, and the only barriers I can envision to the first are
implementor-specific, WG4 *might* acquiesce so long as the additions do not
cause a delay in the production of that next standard.

At the very least this action on my part will ensure that these topics are
on the standards committees' radar screens, and stimulate the discussion and
clarification as to why EXIT PERFORM (with or without CYCLE) was limited to
inline PERFORMs in the 2002 standard.

    -Chuck Stevens
```

#### ↳ Re: Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits

- **From:** Esmond Pitt <esmond.pitt@not.bigpond.com>
- **Date:** 2004-10-30T09:27:14+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<S7Jgd.3709$K7.2223@news-server.bigpond.net.au>`

- **References:** `<clu41g$1t2b$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com>`

```
Chuck Stevens wrote:

  > One of these proposals relaxes the current restriction that an EXIT 
PERFORM
> or EXIT PERFORM CYCLE statement may only appear within a PERFORM ...
> END-PERFORM range.

May I ask why this is needed?
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2004-10-30T14:42:07+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3LNgd.18045$5b1.3902@newssvr17.news.prodigy.com>`
- **References:** `<clu41g$1t2b$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com> <S7Jgd.3709$K7.2223@news-server.bigpond.net.au>`

```
"Esmond Pitt" <esmond.pitt@not.bigpond.com> wrote in message
news:S7Jgd.3709$K7.2223@news-server.bigpond.net.au...
> Chuck Stevens wrote:
>
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> May I ask why this is needed?

Why what is needed, the CYCLE option within a PERFORM.. END-PERFORM loop?
That's there for the reason the PERFORM.. END-PERFORM exists, to offer
complete loop control.

Why you'd want EXIT PERFORM in a PERFORM'd paragraph?  My best guess is that
you'd want this to replace to total obscurity lost when ALTER was dropped.
Supporting this kind of programming is IMNSHO one of the dopiest suggestions
I've ever heard.

MCM
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits

- **From:** "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@mfi.net>
- **Date:** 2004-10-30T11:57:09-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<10o7eij23spll38@corp.supernews.com>`
- **References:** `<clu41g$1t2b$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com> <S7Jgd.3709$K7.2223@news-server.bigpond.net.au>`

```

"Esmond Pitt" <esmond.pitt@not.bigpond.com> wrote in message
news:S7Jgd.3709$K7.2223@news-server.bigpond.net.au...
> Chuck Stevens wrote:
>
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> May I ask why this is needed?

Standard COBOL provides no means, similar to
EXIT PARAGRAPH and EXIT SECTION, to
"short cycle" PERFORM ... THRU without using
a GO TO statement. EXIT PERFORM CYCLE
eliminates those GO TO statements.

The common exception processing feature, added for
2002, is indirect and complex because it uses directives,
declaratives, and exception objects. EXIT PERFORM
provides a means for implementing simple and direct,
localized common exception processing without the
need for directives and declaratives or, for that matter,
additional testing in PERFORM statements.

Following is a pattern for common exception processing
using EXIT PERFORM.

    PERFORM some-process
            UNTIL some-expected-condition
    IF exception-variable NOT = ZERO
        *> common exception handler
    END-IF
    ...

some-process.
    ....
    PERFORM some-other-process
    IF exception-variable NOT = ZERO
        EXIT PERFORM
    END-IF
    ...

some-other-process.
    ...
    IF some-exception-condition
        MOVE some-exception-value TO exception-variable
        EXIT PERFORM
    END-IF
    ...

I would suggest that EXIT PERFORM [CYCLE] for
out-of-line PERFORM is as needful as EXIT PARAGRAPH,
EXIT SECTION, and the common exception processing
feature.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2004-10-30T21:09:59-07:00

- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0410302009.c1781e9@posting.google.com>`

```
> Standard COBOL provides no means, similar to
> EXIT PARAGRAPH and EXIT SECTION, to

> a GO TO statement. EXIT PERFORM CYCLE

…[4 more quoted lines elided]…

          ...
>     END-IF
      .
> I would suggest that EXIT PERFORM [CYCLE] for
> out-of-line PERFORM is as needful as EXIT PARAGRAPH,
> EXIT SECTION, and the common exception processing
> feature.

Possibly, but as I have never felt the need for any of them, or GO TO,
then equally needful is about right.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2004-10-31T09:49:31-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0410310949.6a916128@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<clu41g$1t2b$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com> <S7Jgd.3709$K7.2223@news-server.bigpond.net.au> <10o7eij23spll38@corp.supernews.com> <217e491a.0410302009.c1781e9@posting.google.com> <10o8vebpi1os15d@corp.supernews.com>`

```
"Rick Smith" <ricksmith@mfi.net> wrote 

> > There are other ways to eliminate the GO TOs.
> 
> Not with multiple paragraphs or sections in PERFORM ...
> THRU.

The only purpose of having labels within a PERFORM range is to cater
for a GO TO. If it is your intent to eliminate the GO TOs then the
first thing to do is to eliminate the labels within the range (and
dump the THRU).

>> >     PERFORM some-other-process
>> >     IF exception-variable NOT = ZERO
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>> >     END-IF

> It won't work in all but the last paragraph of a
> PERFORM ... THRU  or PERFORM section and will
> not cause the PERFORM to terminate without a
> test for the same condition.

It will work perfectly if your intent is to eliminate the GO TO.  All
labels within the scope of the perform are unneccessary, drop the THRU
(and SECTION) and comment out or delete the labels then there is no
impediment to using simple logic that does not require any EXIT
pragraph/section/perform.

Granted, with pre-85 coding it was difficult to structure code without
repeating tests because scope termination could only be done by using
a full stop, but that was 20 years ago.

You do not need EXIT ~ to eliminate GO TO, you need to get rid of the
paragraph labels and THRU/SECTION so GO TO does not fit anywhere and
then you will find that EXIT isn't needed either.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@mfi.net>
- **Date:** 2004-10-31T13:46:11-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<10oacqs81h6tk62@corp.supernews.com>`
- **References:** `<clu41g$1t2b$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com> <S7Jgd.3709$K7.2223@news-server.bigpond.net.au> <10o7eij23spll38@corp.supernews.com> <217e491a.0410302009.c1781e9@posting.google.com> <10o8vebpi1os15d@corp.supernews.com> <217e491a.0410310949.6a916128@posting.google.com>`

```

"Richard" <riplin@Azonic.co.nz> wrote in message
news:217e491a.0410310949.6a916128@posting.google.com...
> "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@mfi.net> wrote
>
…[34 more quoted lines elided]…
> then you will find that EXIT isn't needed either.

My response had nothing to do with either *my intent* or
*my need*; it had to do with standard COBOL. Standard
COBOL permits PERFORM ... THRU to have many
paragraphs or sections, permits sections to have many
paragraphs, and permits GO TO statements.

I recognize the truth in what you say; but until WG4 takes
an overt step to deprecate PERFORM ... THRU,
multiple paragraph sections, and GO TO; such as by
make these archaic, then what I have said remains valid
for standard COBOL.

When I discuss standard COBOL, my intent is noble;
that is, I try to rise above the petty; but, in my own use
of COBOL, I embrace the petty. Mr Plinston, you are
preaching to the wrong person.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** Peter Lacey <lacey@mb.sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2004-10-31T14:47:55-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<41854F7B.D2849CDB@mb.sympatico.ca>`
- **References:** `<clu41g$1t2b$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com> <S7Jgd.3709$K7.2223@news-server.bigpond.net.au> <10o7eij23spll38@corp.supernews.com> <217e491a.0410302009.c1781e9@posting.google.com> <10o8vebpi1os15d@corp.supernews.com> <217e491a.0410310949.6a916128@posting.google.com> <10oacqs81h6tk62@corp.supernews.com>`

```
Rick Smith wrote:
> 

> 
> I recognize the truth in what you say; but until WG4 takes
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> preaching to the wrong person.

When I see posts like this, it seems to me that the purpose is to force
the elimination a style of programming (which is perfectly valid,
productive, and reliable) in favour of one which the author of the post
prefers.  The ostensible reason is to eliminate errors which can be
caused by the misuse of the statements involved.  Why not then eliminate
every verb which can be misused?  Consider the COMPUTE verb: if somebody
accidentally uses a "+" instead of a "-", or enters 3.15 instead of 3.14
in his computation - dreadful possibly even catastrophic results can
occur.  Get rid of the thing!!!

My example is facetious, of course, but my point remains valid.  There
are very few verbs in the Cobol canon which can't be misused.  Seems to
me that the argument ought to be applied everywhere.

The misuse of GOTO's only applies during the debugging and testing
phase, anyway.  It isn't as if a GOTO will suddenly misbehave after
years of laying in wait.  If a situation hasn't been anticipated by the
programmer after the same years and the program misfunctions as a
result, it's a design fault, not the fault of the verb in question.

I agree: Djikstra and his acolytes have done us a dubious service.

PL
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2004-10-31T17:52:43-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0410311752.141b49be@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<clu41g$1t2b$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com> <S7Jgd.3709$K7.2223@news-server.bigpond.net.au> <10o7eij23spll38@corp.supernews.com> <217e491a.0410302009.c1781e9@posting.google.com> <10o8vebpi1os15d@corp.supernews.com> <217e491a.0410310949.6a916128@posting.google.com> <10oacqs81h6tk62@corp.supernews.com>`

```
"Rick Smith" <ricksmith@mfi.net> wrote 

> My response had nothing to do with either *my intent* or
> *my need*; 

It did appear that your argument in support of EXIT PERFORM CYCLE was
intended to eliminate GO TO when you posted:

RS>>> EXIT PERFORM CYCLE eliminates those GO TO statements.

> it had to do with standard COBOL. Standard
> COBOL permits PERFORM ... THRU to have many
> paragraphs or sections, permits sections to have many
> paragraphs, and permits GO TO statements.

Cobol also supports the use of completely spagetti code entirely based
on GO TO, this does not mean that enhancements should be added to the
language to promote this use.

My argument against the introduction of out-of-line EXIT PERFORM CYCLE
is that it is not required.
 
> I recognize the truth in what you say; but until WG4 takes
> an overt step to deprecate PERFORM ... THRU,
> multiple paragraph sections, and GO TO; such as by
> make these archaic, then what I have said remains valid
> for standard COBOL.

RS>>> EXIT PERFORM CYCLE eliminates those GO TO statements.

RS>> It won't work in all but the last paragraph of a
RS>> PERFORM ... THRU  or PERFORM section and will
RS>> not cause the PERFORM to terminate without a
RS>> test for the same condition.

What I see here is a conflict in your argument between wanting a
mechanism to "eliminate the GO TOs" and yet retain the structure that
is there merely to support their use.
 
> When I discuss standard COBOL, my intent is noble;
> that is, I try to rise above the petty; but, in my own use
> of COBOL, I embrace the petty. Mr Plinston, you are
> preaching to the wrong person.

I am not 'preaching' at all, simply providing counter arguments to
your assertions.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits

- **From:** Peter Lacey <lacey@mb.sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2004-10-31T00:00:14-05:00

```
> > > > or EXIT PERFORM CYCLE statement may only appear within a PERFORM ...

maintenance; EXIT PERFORM CYCLE does not.
It is progress for those who use PERFORM ... THRU.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2004-10-31T11:30:41-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0410311130.26fc7906@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<clu41g$1t2b$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com> <S7Jgd.3709$K7.2223@news-server.bigpond.net.au> <10o7eij23spll38@corp.supernews.com> <4184715E.6BB3D713@mb.sympatico.ca> <10o8veckfkkvf5f@corp.supernews.com>`

```
"Rick Smith" <ricksmith@mfi.net> wrote 

> A GO TO statement may need to be changed during
> maintenance; 

Under what circumstances would you ever need to change a GO TO during
maintenance?

Given a structure where you have (which I wouldn't reccommend):

     PERFORM A THRU A-Exit

   A.
      ...
          GO TO A-Exit
      ...
   A-Exit.
      EXIT.

What would you change in the 'GO TO A-Exit' ?

Perhaps you mean that when you copy one block of code to a new set of
labels ?

Yes, that is an error-prone change which is a disadvantage of using
THRU or SECTIONs.

> EXIT PERFORM CYCLE does not.
> It is progress for those who use PERFORM ... THRU.

If you retain THRU then there has been no progress.  If you eliminate
the THRU and the A-Exit label then there is no need for EXIT PERFORM
CYCLE, EXIT PARAGRAPH will work (or simply structuring your code
appropriately).

If you have additional labels between A and A-Exit then there are two
possibilities:

   The labels are not referenced, comment them out.

   The labels are referenced by a GO TO, recode without GO TO.

If there are labels to allow for overlapping or subset PERFORM THRUs
then I suggest that you don't try to change anything, ever.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@mfi.net>

```
"Richard" <riplin@Azonic.co.nz> wrote in message
news:217e491a.0410311130.26fc7906@posting.google.com...
> "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@mfi.net> wrote
>
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> maintenance?

Different practices have different maintenance requirements.
This includes practices, in which, "A GO TO statement may
need to be changed during maintenance." At this point, I have
no intention of describing any such practice, since it would
likely result in a litany of reasons why the practice should not
be followed and avoid entirely the point that such COBOL
is valid.

> > EXIT PERFORM CYCLE does not.
> > It is progress for those who use PERFORM ... THRU.
>
> If you retain THRU then there has been no progress.
[snip]

This disparages those who use PERFORM ... THRU, which
is standard COBOL. Mr Plinston, there is no *the* way!
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits

- **From:** Esmond Pitt <esmond.pitt@not.bigpond.com>
- **Date:** 2004-10-31T07:10:42+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Sd0hd.5138$K7.4883@news-server.bigpond.net.au>`
- **In-Reply-To:** `<10o7eij23spll38@corp.supernews.com>`
- **References:** `<clu41g$1t2b$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com> <S7Jgd.3709$K7.2223@news-server.bigpond.net.au> <10o7eij23spll38@corp.supernews.com>`

```
Rick Smith wrote:
> "Esmond Pitt" <esmond.pitt@not.bigpond.com> wrote in message
> news:S7Jgd.3709$K7.2223@news-server.bigpond.net.au...
…[16 more quoted lines elided]…
> eliminates those GO TO statements.

I'm sorry, I have been misunderstood. I was trying to ask why does the 
proposal propose to relax the restriction on the statement appearing 
within a PERFORM ... END-PERFORM range. The reason for the question is 
that the range of a PERFORM statement cannot be determined at compile 
time and in some circumstances not until run-time, according to a letter 
I wrote in the ACS Journal about 18 years ago, but unfortunately I have 
forgotten all the details ... in any case this means that COBOL rules 
about what may only appear inside a PERFORM range are not enforcable by 
the compiler so the restriction is not actually enforcable in any case.

EJP
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Infinite Loops and Explicit Exits

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "Rick Smith" <ricksmith@mfi.net>
- **Date:** 2004-10-31T10:06:12-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<10oa01m8l5oka28@corp.supernews.com>`
- **References:** `<clu41g$1t2b$1@si05.rsvl.unisys.com> <S7Jgd.3709$K7.2223@news-server.bigpond.net.au> <10o7eij23spll38@corp.supernews.com> <Sd0hd.5138$K7.4883@news-server.bigpond.net.au>`

```

"Esmond Pitt" <esmond.pitt@not.bigpond.com> wrote in message
news:Sd0hd.5138$K7.4883@news-server.bigpond.net.au...
> Rick Smith wrote:
> > "Esmond Pitt" <esmond.pitt@not.bigpond.com> wrote in message
…[27 more quoted lines elided]…
> the compiler so the restriction is not actually enforcable in any case.

FDIS, page 447, 14.8.13.2 (EXIT statement) Syntax rules,
"The EXIT PERFORM statement may be specified only
in an inline PERFORM statement."

Perhaps, the confusion is the result of Mr Stevens using
"a PERFORM ... END-PERFORM range" as synonymous
with "inline PERFORM statement." Statements that may
appear within the range of the PERFORM and
END-PERFORM keywords is a distinctly different idea
than statements that may appear within the range of a
PERFORM statement.

FDIS, page 493, 14.8.27.3 (PERFORM statement)
General rules,
"10) The range of a PERFORM statement consists
logically of all those statements that are executed as a result
of executing the PERFORM statement through execution
of the implicit transfer of control to the end of the
PERFORM statement. ..."

The restriction is enforceable, during syntax checking, since
the compiler can know whether it is processing an inline
PERFORM statement, or not.
```
